introduction: In the ever-evolving landscape of virtual healthcare, MediConn Solutions has continuously integrated various third-party wellness applications into our platform. This report analyses the integrations added to our virtual platform in FY2025, their impact on our services, and user engagement trends. The information is vital for our strategic planning and product development efforts moving forward.

subsections.heading: Overview of Third-Party Wellness Applications
subsections.content: Third-party wellness applications have become an integral part of virtual healthcare service delivery. From mental health counseling platforms to fitness tracking tools, they offer diverse functionalities. In FY2020, MediConn integrated 10 new applications, a strategic move that boosted our user base by 30%.
subsections.justification: This content provides historical data about FY2020, not the required FY2025 information.

subsections.heading: User Engagement with New Integrations
subsections.content: The effectiveness of third-party app integrations is reflected in user engagement levels. In Q3 FY2024, an average of 70% of our user base was actively utilizing the integrated apps. This is a significant increase from just 55% in Q1 FY2024, indicating positive user response.
subsections.justification: This content discusses Q3 FY2024 user engagement, not FY2025 third-party app integrations.

subsections.heading: Summary of FY2025 Integration Additions
subsections.content: In FY2025, our technical team worked tirelessly on new integration projects. We streamlined the integration process and reduced average integration time from 10 weeks to 6 weeks. This efficiency improvement had a positive impact on our ability to onboard more third-party applications.
subsections.justification: This content discusses the process and efficiency of integrations, not the number of integrations in FY2025.

conclusion: The integration of third-party wellness applications in FY2025 has significantly broadened our virtual health platform's capabilities. User engagement with these new additions has been positive, indicating a successful implementation strategy. Going forward, we must continue to identify and integrate relevant applications to stay competitive and meet our users' evolving needs. This will be a key focus in our upcoming strategic planning sessions.
